texto: You always say that I’m beautiful. Will you still love me when I’m old?"Geoff’s hand made small circles on his stomach."Elijah." Geoff rarely called him that, and he turned his head to look into Geoff’s eyes. "I don’t love you because you’re beautiful. You’re beautiful because I love you.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Andrew Grey que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “You always say that I’m beautiful. Will you still love me when I’m old?"Geoff’s hand made small c…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, love, sweet, com foco em sweet.

frase_original: You always say that I’m beautiful. Will you still love me when I’m old?"Geoff’s hand made small circles on his stomach."Elijah." Geoff rarely called him that, and he turned his head to look into Geoff’s eyes. "I don’t love you because you’re beautiful. You’re beautiful because I love you.
